items.headline: Nine new homes and seven accessory units planned for Old Trace Road
items.summary: The city is considering a subdivision project at 4103 Old Trace Road that would create nine single-family residences and seven junior accessory dwelling units on a 1-acre lot. This is a notable housing project in a residential estate zone that will go before the full council for approval.

items.headline: Supermicro invests $2.5M in equipment at Rock Avenue facility
items.summary: The tech company is upgrading its San José headquarters with a major $2.5 million equipment installation. This signals continued investment in local manufacturing and engineering operations.

items.headline: City Council approves lease revenue bonds for new Public Safety Building
items.summary: Mountain View is financing a new public safety building through lease revenue bonds. This represents a significant capital investment in police and emergency services infrastructure for the city.

items.headline: City advances Biodiversity and Urban Forest Plan for public review
items.summary: The City Council reviewed a draft Biodiversity and Urban Forest Plan on April 28, signaling potential changes to how Mountain View manages its trees and natural habitat. Residents will likely see this plan move forward for public comment in coming weeks.

items.headline: Zingsters restaurant opening on University Avenue
items.summary: A new restaurant called Zingsters is opening in downtown Palo Alto at 220 University Avenue. The permit for its LED signage suggests an imminent opening of what appears to be a new dining establishment in a prime downtown location.

items.headline: Sunnyvale considers 67-unit mixed-use development with retail
items.summary: The Planning Commission held a study session on a proposed 4.22-acre project that would add 55 townhomes, 12 residential flats, and approximately 18,500 square feet of retail space, including a new two-story building while preserving the existing Speedy's Tacos location. The project would be subdivided into 17 lots and requires a special development permit and tentative map approval.

items.headline: Campbell updates business license tax rates
items.summary: The City Council considered changes to business license tax rates through a municipal code amendment. This annual adjustment affects local businesses operating in Campbell.

items.headline: FIFA World Cup 2026: Giant promotional signs approved for Levi's Stadium area
items.summary: Santa Clara approved a temporary ordinance allowing large supergraphic signs on building surfaces near Levi's Stadium during the 2026 FIFA World Cup events. The signs will be a notable visual change in that area for the duration of the tournament.
